text.title: Spring Boot Layered Architecture Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules for a layered Spring Boot service. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every database-modifying operation MUST run inside a `@Transactional` annotated public method on a @Service bean. Controllers MUST NOT carry `@Transactional`.
rule.rationale: Service-layer @Transactional defines the transactional unit-of-work boundary; controller-level @Transactional leaks HTTP details into persistence concerns.

rule.statement: Dependencies MUST be injected via constructor (final fields). Field injection (`@Autowired` on a field) is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Constructor injection enables immutability, testability without Spring container, and makes circular dependencies visible at startup.

rule.statement: Controller request/response payloads MUST be DTO/record types. JPA `@Entity` classes MUST NOT cross the controller boundary.
rule.rationale: Entities trigger lazy-load exceptions and N+1 issues when serialized; DTOs decouple API contract from schema evolution.

rule.statement: Controller methods accepting request bodies MUST annotate `@Valid` and the DTO MUST use Jakarta Bean Validation annotations (@NotNull, @Size, @Email).
rule.rationale: Pushing validation to a `@ControllerAdvice` ProblemDetail handler gives consistent 400 responses without leaking into service logic.

rule.statement: Every Spring Boot service MUST expose `/actuator/health` (liveness + readiness) and `/actuator/prometheus`. Sensitive endpoints MUST be secured.
rule.rationale: Kubernetes probes + Prometheus scrape are required for SLO measurement; unsecured actuator endpoints leak heap dump / env / beans.
